old_claim.note: Antibiotics provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Immunoassays are defined by biochemical antigen-antibody binding reactions used to detect or quantify analytes.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and basic methodology sections defining immunoassays as bioanalytical methods depending on antigen-antibody reactions.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review states that immunoassay quantitation depends on the reaction of an antigen and an antibody, and describes the method family as bioanalytical.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This directly supports biochemistry as the scoped technical dependency for immunoassay diagnostics, while antibiotics are therapeutic drugs and not a method prerequisite.

ontology_before: Immunoassay diagnostics depended on antibiotics, conflating antibacterial therapeutics with antibody-antigen diagnostic assay chemistry.
ontology_after: Immunoassay diagnostics depend on biochemical antigen-antibody reactions, with antibiotics absent from the diagnostic method lineage.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: immunoassay_diagnostics now depends on biochemistry and no longer directly depends on antibiotics.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to antibiotic susceptibility testing rather than immunoassay diagnostics.
A source showed antibiotics are a technical prerequisite for antibody-antigen assay methods.
A more specific antigen_antibody_binding node is added and replaces the broader biochemistry edge.
short_reason: Immunoassays are antibody-antigen biochemical assays; antibiotics are unrelated as a prerequisite.
